extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE won his 5th Flèche Wallonne attacking 200m from finish on final Mur de Huy ascent, dropping Martin and Teuns after Movistar controlled the race.
tactical_patterns: Movistar controlled through all three Mur passages despite Valverde being solo favorite, revealing willingness to defend repeat winners.
JUNGELS attacked De Marchi 25km out, went solo at 11km, caught at 500m—premature moves futile before final Mur.
GOUGEARD attacked 200m from line but couldn't sustain, proving penultimate attacks fail on Mur's 10% gradient finish.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: 10th win of season at age 36, fifth Flèche title shows Mur de Huy specialists peak later than other classics riders
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: negative: burned energy on 11km solo that was always doomed on Mur finish course profile
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: third podium in four years signals consistency but not breakthrough capacity on Mur's steepest sections
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: first major classics podium suggests emerging capacity for explosive short climbs
